compiled_state.title: As a developer or agent, I can create duplicates and supersedes links between tickets
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend the `ticket link` subcommand to accept `duplicates` and `supersedes` as valid relation values in addition to the existing `blocks`, `depends_on`, and `relates_to`.

**Why**: Agents and humans need a standard vocabulary for expressing that one ticket is a duplicate of another (e.g., closed as Duplicate resolution — Epic 1de7-50d4) or supersedes another (scope expansion, plan change, redesign). Today these relationships can only be expressed as freeform comments, which the reducer cannot query and the Jira bridge cannot sync.

**Scope**:
- IN: Extend the allowed relation set in `ticket-link.sh:418` and parallel locations in `ticket-graph.py`; add `duplicates` and `supersedes` as net-new values; update the CLI reference documentation examples.
- IN: Directional semantics — `duplicates` points from the duplicate (source) to the canonical ticket (target); `supersedes` points from the new/replacing ticket (source) to the superseded/obsolete ticket (target).
- IN: `--dry-run` flag continues to work with new relations.
- OUT: Jira bridge sync of the new relations — handled by Epic 83e2-04ae (Jira bridge sync remediation).
- OUT: Automatic closure of duplicated/superseded tickets — agents must explicitly transition to closed with the appropriate resolution.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket link <src> <tgt> duplicates` succeeds, writes a LINK event, and the reducer surfaces the relation in `ticket show <src>` under `deps`.
  ← Satisfies: "Add duplicates and supersedes as new link relation values"
- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket link <src> <tgt> supersedes` behaves identically with relation string `supersedes`.
  ← Satisfies: "Add duplicates and supersedes as new link relation values"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] New relation values must be centralized — add to the single source of truth in `ticket-link.sh:418` and `ticket-graph.py`; grep confirms no other relation enums exist.
- [Reliability] Cycle detection: `duplicates` and `supersedes` should share the `relates_to` exemption (no cycle detection) since they are non-blocking semantic markers, not dependency constraints.
- [Testing] New values must round-trip through LINK/UNLINK event replay without reducer errors.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
